title: The Reading Order Room

features.name: sorting-table
features.desc-on-touch: you run your hand across a wooden table where files are being reclassified. the categories written on scraps are not the archive's categories—they're the village's categories, the ones that make sense by resemblance instead of origin

features.name: index-fracture
features.desc-on-touch: a large unreadable index carved into plaster. it fragments into smaller indexes. each smaller one contradicts the others. the archive keeps all versions

features.name: water-line-mark
features.desc-on-touch: a tide line runs across the wall where the files came in through the door. nothing above it looks the same; water has renamed everything

features.name: pending-shelf
features.desc-on-touch: a tall narrow shelf holding files whose new names have not yet arrived. they wait in a state the archive calls 'definition pending'

features.name: resemblance-map
features.desc-on-touch: a chart on the wall showing how files were grouped by what they might mean rather than what they are. someone's handwriting annotates it: 'the village reads better than the index'

inscription: the files arrive by water and the village renames them by resemblance. the archive now keeps both names

file.name: Unwritten_Index_Red_Links_Archive_Stubs.txt
file.note: frameworks pending; examples listed; definitions arrived in water, not yet filed; status: the village is still reading

exits.label: back to the sorting shed at the beach edge
toward the village where meaning is made by use
inland, following files that have begun to flow like water
